channel.title: NYT > Most Recent Headlines
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: King Charles and Queen Camilla to Visit New York’s 9/11 Memorial
item.description: After attending a ceremony at the memorial in Lower Manhattan, the royal couple is scheduled to visit a Harlem nonprofit, the New York Public Library’s main branch and Christie’s.
item.dc:creator: Jin Yu Young
item.category: Charles III, King of the United Kingdom
Camilla, Queen Consort of the United Kingdom

media:content.media:credit: Vincent Alban/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The south tower reflecting pool at the World Trade Center site in Manhattan last year.

item.title: Un chatbot de IA ayuda a cuidar a personas mayores en Corea del Sur
item.description: Esta nación envejece más rápido que ningún otro país. Como no cuentan con trabajadores sociales o cuidadores familiares suficientes, la IA ayuda a llenar parte del vacío.
item.dc:creator: Choe Sang-Hun

media:content.media:credit: Woohae Cho for The New York Times

item.title: Picture Prompt: A.I. Artist?
item.description: Is it OK to use artificial intelligence to create art?
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

item.title: A City Hall Reporter Reveals Where the Scoops Come From
item.description: Sally Goldenberg covers New York City politics and government for The Times, attending to a network of sources more than 20 years in the making.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Bahr

media:content.media:credit: Juan Arredondo for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Sally Goldenberg, a former New York City Hall reporter at Politico, covers the administration of Mayor Zohran Mamdani for The Times.

item.title: Today’s Spelling Bee Hints for April 29, 2026
item.description: Feeling stuck on today’s puzzle? We can help.
item.dc:creator: New York Times Games

item.title: ¿Por qué las películas parecen cada vez más oscuras?
item.description: A menudo, los espectadores se preguntan por qué las películas actuales se ven más oscuras que las de hace años. He aquí el diagnóstico de algunos expertos.
item.dc:creator: Jessica M. Goldstein

item.title: Palabra del día en inglés: ‘placate’
item.description: Esta palabra ha aparecido en 153 artículos en NYTimes.com en el último año. ¿Puedes usarla en una frase?
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

item.title: Word of the Day: placate
item.description: This word has appeared in 126 articles on NYTimes.com in the past year. Can you use it in a sentence?
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

item.title: Jimmy Kimmel Spills the Tea on Trump’s Royal Meeting
item.description: The late night show host called the king of England “one of the very few people who might actually be able to tip things even slightly in a positive direction.”
item.dc:creator: Trish Bendix
item.category: The Daily Show with Jon Stewart (TV Program)

item.title: Takeaways From the California Governor Debate at Pomona College
item.description: The candidates felt pressure to differentiate themselves, with ballots going out to voters next week. The result was a more spirited battle than past debates.
item.dc:creator: Laurel Rosenhall and Soumya Karlamangla

media:content.media:credit: Mario Tama/Getty Images
media:content.media:description: Eight candidates took the stage at Pomona College in one of the final debates in the California gubernatorial primary race.

item.title: La popularidad de Trump cae de cara a las elecciones intermedias
item.description: Aún faltan seis meses para las elecciones, y algunos miembros del partido del presidente afirman que aún hay tiempo para enderezar el rumbo, pese al panorama desalentador.
item.dc:creator: Shane Goldmacher

media:content.media:credit: Kenny Holston/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Las perspectivas del Partido Republicano han empeorado, al igual que la confianza de los votantes en que el presidente Trump pueda solucionar los problemas de la economía.

item.title: I Played Putin in a War Game. He Could Invade Again.
item.description: NATO countries need to get their act together.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Gabuev

item.title: At State Dinner, King Charles Charms the Court of Trump
item.description: King Charles III presented President Trump with a golden bell. “Should you ever need to get hold of us,” the king said, “well, just give us a ring!”
item.dc:creator: Shawn McCreesh
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
Charles III, King of the United Kingdom

media:content.media:credit: Kenny Holston/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: King Charles III at the state dinner at the White House on Tuesday night, delivering a speech with the right ingredients for President Trump's second term.

item.title: How Iran Accumulated 11 Tons of Enriched Uranium
item.description: Two months after the United States began a war meant to prevent Iran from ever building an atomic bomb, the fate of its stockpile of enriched uranium remains a mystery.
item.dc:creator: Blacki Migliozzi and William J. Broad

media:content.media:credit: The New York Times

item.title: Gallows Humor Provides Respite From Mideast Wars
item.description: Arab digital creators on social media are turning out edgy material to cope with the conflicts and bring levity to a pain that often feels too raw.
item.dc:creator: Abdi Latif Dahir

media:content.media:credit: Diego Ibarra Sánchez for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Samer Moumneh, wearing a red headband, recording a new sketch in a cafe in Beirut, Lebanon, last week.

item.title: Global Deforestation Slows, W.R.I. Report Finds. But Wildfires Are Taking a Toll.
item.description: In 2025, the world razed less forest than any other year in the last decade. The bad news: global warming is making wildfires more frequent and intense.
item.dc:creator: Sachi Kitajima Mulkey and Harry Stevens

media:content.media:credit: The New York Times

item.title: Corrections: April 29, 2026
item.description: Corrections that appeared in print on Wednesday, April 29, 2026.

item.title: Quote of the Day: In Months, China Turns Reef Into Possible Base
item.description: Quotation of the Day for Wednesday, April 29, 2026.

item.title: Acid Attack in Indonesia Evokes Brutality of Suharto Era
item.description: The victim had protested against the widening role of the military, which activists say is reminiscent of the country’s decades-long dictatorship.
item.dc:creator: Muktita Suhartono and Richard C. Paddock
item.category: Human Rights and Human Rights Violations

media:content.media:credit: Bay Ismoyo/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
media:content.media:description: A mural featuring Andrie Yunus, the deputy coordinator of the Kontras rights group, who was the victim of an acid attack, in Jakarta, Indonesia.

item.title: Mamdani’s Candidate Concedes in High-Profile Council Race in Manhattan
item.description: The City Council special election pitting Lindsey Boylan, backed by Mayor Zohran Mamdani, against Carl Wilson, a community activist, was not officially called. But Ms. Boylan conceded.
item.dc:creator: Liam Stack

media:content.media:credit: Graham Dickie for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: tk

item.title: Oakland’s Airport Can Use ‘San Francisco’ in Its Name Under Settlement
item.description: The agreement ends a two-year dispute between the Bay Area cities over the smaller airport’s rebranding.
item.dc:creator: Pooja Salhotra

media:content.media:credit: Justin Sullivan/Getty Images
media:content.media:description: San Francisco had sued over the Oakland airport name in 2024.

item.title: NYT Crossword Answers: April 29, 2026
item.description: Joseph Gangi shares a great Wednesday puzzle.
item.dc:creator: Isaac Aronow

media:content.media:credit: Johan Ordonez/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images

item.title: Dirk Kempthorne, Former Idaho Governor and Interior Secretary, Dies at 74
item.description: A Republican, Mr. Kempthorne rose to prominence as the mayor of Boise in the late 1980s and early ’90s, as the city became a haven for outdoor enthusiasts.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Ziegler

media:content.media:credit: Brendan Hoffman for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Dirk Kempthorne testifying in 2008. Before his tenure as interior secretary, he was elected as the governor of Idaho in 1998 and remained in office until 2006.

item.title: White House Urges House to Quickly Fund D.H.S.
item.description: The call amounted to a rebuke of Speaker Mike Johnson, who has delayed action on a homeland security spending bill and suggested this week that it needed changes.
item.dc:creator: Michael Gold

media:content.media:credit: Salwan Georges for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The memo calls for the “immediate passage” of a stalled bill to reopen the Department of Homeland Security.

item.title: Full Guest List for Trump’s State Dinner With Charles and Camilla
item.description: Six members of the Supreme Court were invited to the dinner, alongside top administration officials, billionaires and Republican lawmakers.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times
item.category: Charles III, King of the United Kingdom
Camilla, Queen Consort of the United Kingdom

media:content.media:credit: Kenny Holston/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: President Trump and the first lady, Melania Trump, welcomed King Charles III of Britain and Queen Camilla to the White House on Tuesday evening for a state dinner.

item.title: Trump Administration Issues Another Indictment Against Comey
item.description: The Trump administration announced on Tuesday that it was pursuing charges against James Comey, the former F.B.I. director, for a deleted Instagram post displaying seashells arranged in a way that the administration claimed threatened the president.
item.dc:creator: Jamie Leventhal

media:content.media:credit: Monica Jorge for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: James B. Comey, the former F.B.I. director, was indicted last year by a grand jury in Virginia, accused of lying and obstructing a congressional investigation over testimony he gave in 2020. The charge was later dismissed.

item.title: See the Menu for the British Royal State Dinner
item.description: Spring-herbed ravioli and Dover sole meunière are among the dishes at the dinner honoring King Charles III of Britain and Queen Camilla.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times
item.category: Charles III, King of the United Kingdom
Camilla, Queen Consort of the United Kingdom

media:content.media:credit: Kenny Holston/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: A dinner menu for the evening, seen at the White House.

item.title: King Charles III Delivers Message of Unity to Congress
item.description: King Charles III delivered an address to a joint meeting of Congress on Tuesday, becoming only the second British monarch to do so.
item.dc:creator: Jackeline Luna
item.category: Charles III, King of the United Kingdom

media:content.media:credit: Henry Nicholls/Agence France-Presse, via Getty Images

item.title: Starbucks Reports Strong Store Traffic and Rise in Quarterly Sales
item.description: The chain’s chief executive credited its turnaround plan for improved U.S. same-store sales, but said higher gas and utility costs were a concern.
item.dc:creator: Julie Creswell

media:content.media:credit: Nico Schinco for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Global sales for Starbucks stores open at least a year rose 6.2 percent in the latest quarter compared with a year earlier.

item.title: Man Accuses N.Y.P.D. Detectives in Legal Filing of Brutal Assault
item.description: Timothy Brown said he had been humiliated and injured after he was punched and kicked by police during a wrongful arrest that was caught on video.
item.dc:creator: Maia Coleman

media:content.media:credit: Mary Altaffer/Associated Press

item.title: Beneath King Charles’s Jokes and Decorum, Some Subtle Rebuttals to Trump
item.description: King Charles III gently pushed back against President Trump’s attacks on Britain and NATO, and he spoke of the importance of checks and balances.
item.dc:creator: Michael D. Shear and Zolan Kanno-Youngs
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
Charles III, King of the United Kingdom

media:content.media:credit: Salwan Georges for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: King Charles III addressing Congress on Tuesday.

item.title: King Charles III Cracks Jokes and Wins Over Congress
item.description: Charles III’s address at a joint meeting of Congress on Tuesday sprinkled well-crafted jokes among carefully chosen references to the United States and Britain’s shared history.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Ahn
item.category: Charles III, King of the United Kingdom

media:content.media:credit: Salwan Georges for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: King Charles received multiple standing ovations and rounds of laughter during his address to a joint meeting of Congress in Washington on Tuesday.

item.title: Canadá ofrece una nueva forma de obtener la ciudadanía
item.description: Las autoridades han abierto una vía hacia la ciudadanía para quienes puedan demostrar que tienen un antepasado nacido en Canadá. Millones de personas podrían optar a ella.
item.dc:creator: Vjosa Isai and Matina Stevis-Gridneff

media:content.media:credit: Andrej Ivanov/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
media:content.media:description: Una ceremonia de ciudadanía para nuevos canadienses el año pasado en Montreal

item.title: Father and Daughter Who Sold Fake Warhols Plead Guilty in Forgery Scheme
item.description: Erwin Bankowski and Karolina Bankowska, of New Jersey, commissioned a Polish artist to create counterfeit artworks that sold for a total of over $2 million.
item.dc:creator: Santul Nerkar

item.title: OpenAI Trial Starts With Two Very Different Tales of a Company’s Early Years
item.description: In the trial’s first day of testimony, Elon Musk said greed led co-founder Sam Altman to pull the A.I. lab away from its nonprofit roots. OpenAI says that’s nonsense.
item.dc:creator: Cade Metz and Mike Isaac

media:content.media:credit: Brennan Smart for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Elon Musk arriving at the Ronald V. Dellums U.S. Courthouse in Oakland Tuesday.

item.title: King Charles Stresses Significance of U.S.-U.K. Ties
item.description: Also, James Comey, the former F.B.I. director, is indicted. Here’s the latest at the end of Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Cullen

media:content.media:credit: Salwan Georges for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: King Charles III addresses Congress today.

item.title: 4 extranjeros estaban en el operativo antidroga de Chihuahua, dicen autoridades de México
item.description: Autoridades mexicanas dieron a conocer que había más extranjeros presentes en la redada antinarcóticos tras la que murieron dos hombres identificados como agentes de la CIA.
item.dc:creator: Paulina Villegas

media:content.media:credit: Fiscalía General de Justicia de Chihuahua
media:content.media:description: Un presunto laboratorio de metanfetamina en una zona remota del estado de Chihuahua, México.

item.title: Port Authority to Install Tracker Not Used During Deadly LaGuardia Crash
item.description: Federal investigators had said that the lack of a transponder on a fire truck played a role in a deadly collision with an Air Canada Express jet at LaGuardia.
item.dc:creator: Patrick McGeehan
item.category: Port Authority of New York and New Jersey

media:content.media:credit: Dave Sanders for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: A fire truck responding to an emergency call from another plane collided with an Air Canada Express jet at LaGuardia Airport on March 22.

item.title: Prime Minister Mark Carney Says Canada’s Economy Is Expected to Grow and Deficit to Fall
item.description: Prime Minister Mark Carney’s budget update keeps to his path of diversifying away from the United States.
item.dc:creator: Ian Austen

media:content.media:credit: Sean Kilpatrick/The Canadian Press, via Associated Press
media:content.media:description: Prime Minister Mark Carney of Canada arriving at Parliament Hill in Ottawa on Tuesday.

item.title: Severe Storms Sweep the South, With Possible Tornadoes and Large Hail
item.description: Some damage was reported in Texas on Tuesday evening as storms raced across the Central United States.
item.dc:creator: Amy Graff and Jesus Jiménez

media:content.media:credit: The New York Times

item.title: Soldier Pleads Not Guilty in $400,000 Betting Case Over Maduro’s Ouster
item.description: Master Sergeant Gannon Ken Van Dyke appeared in federal court in Manhattan, where he was accused of using classified information about Nicolás Maduro’s ouster to make money on Polymarket.
item.dc:creator: Wes Parnell

media:content.media:credit: Jefferson Siegel for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Master Sergeant Gannon Ken Van Dyke made over $400,000 placing bets on Polymarket related to the capture of Nicolás Maduro, according to prosecutors and an unsealed indictment.

item.title: ‘Dances With Wolves’ Actor Is Sentenced to Life in Prison
item.description: Nathan Chasing Horse had been convicted of charges including the sexual assault of women and girls and the possession of child sexual abuse imagery.
item.dc:creator: Reggie Ugwu

media:content.media:credit: John Locher/Associated Press
media:content.media:description: Nathan Chasing Horse, in court in January. He had pleaded not guilty to all charges against him.

item.title: The E.P.A.’s Lost Science
item.description: The agency’s prestigious research office is being dismantled by the Trump administration, a plan to dam the Bering Strait and more climate news.
item.dc:creator: Lisa Friedman

media:content.media:credit: Cornell Watson for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Bryan Hubbell, the former head of the air, climate and energy program at the Environmental Protection Agency’s research office. “The state of science is struggling,” he said.

item.title: Dirty Gold
item.description: A Times investigation shows how the U.S. and Canadian mints ended up buying gold that came from a Colombian drug cartel.
item.dc:creator: Katrin Bennhold

media:content.media:credit: Federico Rios for The New York Times

item.title: Democrats Pitch ‘New Affordability,’ Looking to Widen Midterm Appeal
item.description: Progressives who expect their party to take control of Congress are putting forth a slate of bills aimed at helping lower costs for Americans, financed by tax increases on the wealthy.
item.dc:creator: Annie Karni

media:content.media:credit: Ilana Panich-Linsman for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Representative Greg Casar, Democrat of Texas, during a protest in Austin last year. Mr. Casar and the Congressional Progressive Caucus are trying to show voters that Democrats have their own policy solutions beyond simply dismantling President Trump’s agenda.

item.title: The Banality of Evil, Again
item.description: The distinguishing feature of Cole Tomas Allen’s manifesto is its insipidity.
item.dc:creator: Bret Stephens

media:content.media:credit: Kylie Cooper/Reuters

item.title: Deal Talks Between Pernod and Maker of Jack Daniel’s Falter
item.description: The collapse of the talks could pave the way for another spirits company, Sazerac, to open negotiations with Brown-Forman.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Hirsch

media:content.media:credit: Scott Olson/Getty Images
media:content.media:description: Brown-Forman, which makes Jack Daniel’s whiskey, and Pernod Ricard did not give reasons for the failure of their merger talks.

item.title: Trump’s Face Will Appear on Limited-Edition U.S. Passports, State Dept. Says
item.description: The department said it planned to release “a limited number of specially designed” U.S. passports that feature a picture of the president to commemorate the country’s 250th anniversary in July.
item.dc:creator: Taylor Robinson

media:content.media:credit: U.S. Department of State
media:content.media:description: An image of the proposed limited-edition passport design released by the State Department.

item.title: After Decades of Resistance, a Casino Finally Opens in New York City
item.description: As Aqueduct Racetrack winds down, Resorts World opens the first full-scale commercial casino in Queens, with live table games, dealers and cards.
item.dc:creator: Corey Kilgannon

media:content.media:credit: Vincent Alban/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: KT Lim, the chief executive of Genting Berhad, the parent company of Resorts World, performs a ceremonial dice throw alongside the rapper Nas at New York City’s first casino.

item.title: Trump’s Dinner for King Charles Celebrates an Enduring Alliance
item.description: King Charles III and President Trump praised the Anglo-American relationship in lofty terms, with disagreements about the Iran war left mostly unmentioned.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times

media:content.media:credit: Kenny Holston/The New York Times
